title: Saladin — to the Horns of Hattin

intro.title: The Loss of the True Cross
intro.body: Saladin crossed the Jordan in late June 1187 with 30,000 men, drew the Crusader army into waterless ground at the Horns of Hattin, and shattered it on 4 July. Jerusalem surrendered three months later. Ibn al-Athir and Imad al-Din are the primary Muslim sources.

stops.name: Damascus
stops.note: Saladin's headquarters since 1174. Spring 1187: he summons the contingents of his empire from Egypt to Mesopotamia.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Ayyubid Damascus citadel, contingents of Egyptian and Syrian cavalry assembling outside.

stops.name: Tell Ashtara
stops.note: Saladin musters the field army on the Hauran plain south of Damascus, late June 1187.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Black-tented camp on a wide basalt plain, banners of green and gold, horse-lines.

stops.name: Jordan crossing (al-Sennabra)
stops.note: Saladin fords the Jordan south of the Sea of Galilee on 30 June 1187.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. River ford, cavalry crossing with banners, Sea of Galilee visible upstream.

stops.name: Horns of Hattin
stops.note: 4 July 1187. Saladin pins the Crusader army against the twin-peaked extinct volcano without water; the army melts in the heat. King Guy and the True Cross are captured.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Twin-peaked volcanic hill above dry plain, smoke from burning brush, Crusader banners falling.

stops.name: Jerusalem
stops.note: Surrenders 2 October 1187 after a brief siege. Balian of Ibelin negotiates the terms; Saladin allows most Christians to ransom themselves.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Dome of the Rock visible above walls, mounted sultan in green entering Jaffa Gate, refugees streaming out the opposite side.
